title: race detector complains about   go.podman.io/storage/pkg/lockfile.(*LockFile).lock()
body:
### Issue Description

While debugging a race condition, I tried running podman 6-dev  (1090a09da81d5b41d8bd48bb969d49ec795ec866) with -race.

I got the following stacktrace,

```
==================
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

This happened while running `sudo podman system service  
 unix://$SOCKET` and running docker-ce-client 29.1 against said socket.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
I self-compiled with:

`go build -race       -buildvcs=false         -o /tmp/podman         -ldflags "-X github.com/containers/podman/v6/libpod/define.gitCommit=engflow-superproject-$(git rev-parse HEAD) -X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/define.engflowVersion=${DEBIAN_VERSION} -X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/config._etcDir=/etc -X github.com/containers/podman/v5/pkg/systemd/quadlet._binDir=/usr/local/bin -X github.com/containers/common/pkg/config.additionalHelperBinariesDir= "         -tags "apparmor systemd exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper seccomp "         ./cmd/podman; sudo cp /tmp/podman /usr/local/bin/podman`
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Thanks for the ping.

At a glance, this looks real (… enough): `LockFile.lock` _always_ updates `l.locked` and other fields. So, one goroutine can lock for reading, and be inside `AssertLocked`, while another goroutine can be inside `lock` and writing to these fields.

I.e. I think the part oft the comment saying “with no possible writers” is not true. Now, the writes must be writing an already-present value, so it should be safe at the assembly level, but the unsynchronized R+W _is_ a violation of the Go memory model. I think https://go.dev/ref/mem#restrictions does mean that we are guaranteed to see the correct value.

Still, moving the initialization of the non-counter values into the `count == 0` cases would make the correctness unambiguous — _and_ perhaps save a few nanoseconds :)
